REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DECISION ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER SULLA JR., ANUNCIO L. R.N.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ANUNCIO L. SULLA, JR., R.N. PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. - AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER'S WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. THE MATTER IS REMANDED TO RESPONDENT TO CLEAR UP THE DISCREPANCY BETWEEN ITS FINDING THAT THERE WAS NO SUBSTANTIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CRIME AND THE PROFESSION AND ITS FINDING OF A VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 2762. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED BECAUSE THE BOARD ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. PETITIONER TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Re Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER ANUNCIO L. SULLA, JR., R.N. PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE.

APPEARANCES: Adam G. Slote, Esq. of Slote & Links 393-8012 for petitioner & moving party; Jonathan D. Cooper, Deputy Atty General 703-1404 for respondent & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Petitioner's writ of mandate is granted. The matter is remanded to respondent to clear up the discrepancy between its finding that there was no substantial relationship between the crime and the profession and its finding of a violation of Business & Professions code section 2762. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and costs is denied because the Board acted with substantial justification. Petitioner to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR 689

Order To Show Cause Re Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar May-10-2011 Continued To Jun-02-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE WRIT OF MANDATE.
